title: salix × straehleri
intro: Salix × straehleri, also known as Straehleri's willow, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between the Salix purpurea and the Salix viminalis. The medicinal properties of Salix × straehleri lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of using Salix × straehleri include relief from pain, reduction of inflammation, and improvement of cardiovascular health, as well as potential antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Salix × straehleri are typically made from its bark, which can be dried and used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
